response: 4957 is a prime number. It has no divisors other than 1 and itself among primes less than its square root (~70.4).
reasoning_content: We need to check if 4957 is prime. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. 4957 mod 2 = 1, odd. mod 3: sum digits 4+9+5+7=25, not divisible by 3. mod 5: last digit not 0 or 5. mod 7: 7*708=4956, remainder 1, so not divisible. mod 11: alternating sum: 4-9+5-7 = -7, not multiple of 11. mod 13: 13*381=4953, remainder 4. mod 17: 17*291=4947, remainder 10. mod 19: 19*261=4959, too high; 19*260=4940, remainder 17. mod 23: 23*215=4945, remainder 12. mod 29: 29*171=4959, too high; 29*170=4930, remainder 27. mod 31: 31*160=4960, too high; 31*159=4929, remainder 28. mod 37: 37*134=4958, remainder -1, so 4957 mod 37 = 36? Actually 37*134=4958, so 4957 = 4958-1, remainder 36. Not zero. mod 41: 41*121=4961, too high; 41*120=4920, remainder 37. mod 43: 43*115=4945, remainder 12. mod 47: 47*105=4935, remainder 22. sqrt(4957) ~ 70.4, so we need to check primes up to 70. Next primes: 53, 59, 61, 67, 71. 53*93=4929, remainder 28. 59*84=4956, remainder 1. 61*81=4941, remainder 16. 67*74=4958? Actually 67*74=4958, remainder -1, so 4957 mod 67 = 66? 67*74=4958, so 4957 is 1 less, remainder 66. Not zero. Next prime 71 > sqrt? sqrt(4957) ~ 70.4, so 71 > sqrt, so we only need to check primes up to 67. None divided evenly. So 4957 is prime. But quick sanity: check for 7 gave remainder 1, 13 gave 4, etc. No small factors. So it's prime.
